task: V3.15 Low-Prevalence Screening Simulation + Natural Distribution Stress Test
best_supported_prevalence_range: 10%-25% (marginal at 10% with FP/TP=12, viable at 25% with FP/TP=3.8); below 10% requires two-stage pipeline (R4 -> LLM/human), not standalone

fp_tp_at_1pct.verdict: NOT acceptable as standalone screener (FP/TP far above medical screening range 5-10)

fp_tp_at_3pct.verdict: NOT acceptable as standalone screener (FP/TP above medical screening range 5-10)

fp_tp_at_5pct.verdict: BORDERLINE (FP/TP above typical medical screening range 5-10); may be acceptable only for very-high-risk applications

fp_tp_at_10pct.verdict: MARGINAL (FP/TP at upper edge of medical screening range 5-10); viable for high-risk safety-critical screening only

recommended_paper_claim: R4 has boundary-recognition ability on the balanced SimClaim diagnostic set (strong_F1 = 0.40, above chance) and its recall is stable (~0.36) across simulated prevalences from 1% to 25%. However, R4 is NOT viable as a standalone screener below 10% prevalence (FP/TP = 25-121, precision = 0.8-3.9%). At low prevalence, R4 retains value as a first-stage high-recall filter that captures strong_action_overclaim cases missed by all three LLM judges (3.19-80.04 per 1000 samples across prevalences). LLM judges exhibit a high-accuracy / low-recall failure mode at low prevalence (99% accuracy, 4.3% recall at 1%), making accuracy a misleading deployment metric. The recommended deployment configuration is a two-stage pipeline (R4 -> LLM/human), proposed but not evaluated in this paper. Balanced SimClaim macro-F1 cannot be extrapolated to deployment; prevalence-aware metrics (FP/TP, NNR, review burden) are required for deployment-stage evaluation. All simulation results are on silver labels and use a heuristic 80/15/5 non-strong composition; gold validation and natural-prevalence corpus studies are required before deployment claims.
main_risk: Three-fold: (1) Base pool is only 100 matched samples (25 strong_action cases), limiting diversity of strong_action patterns; (2) non-strong composition 80/15/5 is a heuristic approximation, NOT empirically validated against a real natural-prevalence corpus, so absolute FP/TP values may shift with real distribution; (3) all results are on silver labels (pre-gold), and gold adjudication (frozen protocol, annotation NOT begun) may shift the base predictions and therefore the simulation conclusions. Additional risk: the two-stage pipeline (R4 -> LLM/human) is proposed but NOT empirically evaluated; the paper must not claim it as a validated configuration.
next_required_experiment: Two parallel tracks required before deployment claims: (1) Gold pilot annotation per gold_pilot_protocol_freeze_v1 (PROTOCOL FROZEN, annotation NOT begun) on 50-sample pilot, then re-evaluate R4/LLM on gold-validated subset and re-run low-prevalence simulation with gold labels; (2) Natural-prevalence corpus study on real scientific writing (not counterfactual SimClaim) to empirically validate the 80/15/5 non-strong composition and the 1%-10% strong_action prevalence range. Optional but recommended: (3) Empirically evaluate the two-stage pipeline (R4 -> LLM/human) on a held-out set to validate the proposed deployment configuration.

quality_checks.base_data: 100 matched samples from gpt_vs_r4_deepseek_comparison.csv (25 per class)

file_inventory.low_prevalence_sample_manifest.csv: Bootstrap sample manifest (7000 rows = 7 prevalences x 1000 iterations)
file_inventory.low_prevalence_screening_metrics.csv: Per-method per-prevalence screening metrics (28 rows = 4 methods x 7 prevalences)
file_inventory.low_prevalence_screening_summary.json: Full summary with config, methods, results_by_prevalence
file_inventory.review_budget_curve.csv: Review budget curve (224 rows = 4 methods x 7 prevalences x 8 budgets)
file_inventory.balanced_vs_low_prevalence_interpretatio: Interpretation guide for balanced vs low-prevalence settings
